name: Billy May
abstract: Edward William May Jr. (November 10, 1916 – January 22, 2004) was an American composer, arranger and trumpeter.

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: Billy May was probably my best friend in the whole music business.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Billy May brought an arrangement in back in 1939 or '40... I said, 'I haven't got a soprano, Billy.' And he said, 'Well, go buy one, the part's here.' And I did.

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The very first album. Billy May’s. We sent those Ray Bryant demos out... Billy just said, “Okay m’am, you got it,” and brought back in new charts the next day.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Capp
about_quotes.evidence: after that band I did several other road bands — Neal Hefti’s band and Billy May, Charlie Barnet; and I worked with a lot of singers
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: Meanwhile I was doing things with Nelson Riddle and Billy May

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: I made that tribute to Lunceford with Billy May. Billy May transcribed all the arrangements off the records... And in the meantime, I was doing all the rest of this studio work -- jingles and record dates and Nelson Riddle and Billy May and you name it
